title: Unit 4 Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass: Regular Verbs and Negation

script.text: Welcome... to the Learn Greek Grammar Masterclass for Unit 4: Regular Verbs and Negation. In the last unit... we met 'Being' and 'Having'. But the real power of the Greek language lies in its regular verbs. They follow a strict pattern... a rhythm that... once you master it... unlocks thousands of actions. Athina... what is the secret to recognizing a Greek verb in its natural state?
In Greek... we don’t have an 'Infinitive' like 'To Walk'. Instead... dictionaries use the 'I' form. And for most verbs... that means they end in a simple...
So, just like in 'To Be'... the ending tells the whole story. Let's look at the patterns for the most common group: Group A. These are verbs like
for 'I read'. Athina... let's hear the endings for 'I', 'You', 'He', 'We', 'You all', and 'They'.
It's like a song! 'O... is... ee. Oume... ete... oun'. Let’s apply it to the verb 'To Drink'.
Πίνω... Πίνεις... Πίνει. Πίνουμε... Πίνετε... Πίνουν.
Wait, I heard something familiar. The 'They' form ended in
Exactly. And just like 'To Have'... in the street you will often hear an extra
Now... what if we want to say what we AREN'T doing? Negation. In English... we need 'don't' or 'doesn't'. It’s complicated. In Greek?
It is so simple. Just add the word
before the verb. That’s it! Listen:
ever change its shape like the article
Yes! It follows the exact same 'Disappearing Nu' rule. We keep the
only before vowels or hard sounds like K, P, or T. So we say...
Got it. Keep the 'N' if the next word is 'hard'. Now... what about when we want to say 'AND'? It’s such a small but vital word.
But be careful... if the next word starts with a vowel... we often squish it together.
It flows much better. This has been another great session. You've mastered the regular 'O' endings and the simple power of

for negation. You can now build thousands of dynamic sentences. Practice with the common verbs list and try making your own negative sentences tonight. Excellent work... we'll see you in Unit 5: Objects and Direction.
